title: A skill you wish more people learned at school
summary: Argue for one practical skill that you think schools should teach more carefully.
topic: A skill schools should teach more carefully
prompt: Write 3 short paragraphs (150–200 words total) arguing that one specific skill — not exercise, not coding — should be taught more carefully in schools. Use opinion phrases ('I think…', 'In my view…') and modal verbs ('should', 'could', 'would'). Answer at least one objection.
hintStarters: A class on this would not be…
sampleText:
I think every student should learn how to listen well before they leave school. We are taught to write essays, to give presentations, and to defend our ideas, but no one really teaches us how to sit in a room and listen to someone we disagree with. As a result, many adults are loud but lonely. They cannot have a real conversation.

A class on listening would not be difficult. Students could practise repeating what their classmates said before answering. They could learn to ask one more question instead of jumping in with their opinion. Over time, this would change the way they talk to family members, colleagues, and even strangers on the bus.

Some people will say that listening is a personal habit, not a school subject. I disagree. We learn to read, even though we use our eyes every day. We can also learn to listen, even though we have always had ears. The earlier we start, the kinder the world becomes.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bảo vệ ý kiến của chúng ta
chen ngang với ý kiến của họ
thế giới càng trở nên tử tế hơn

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Modal verbs for opinion and possibility
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'should learn', 'could practise', 'would change' diễn đạt đề xuất, khả năng và kết quả giả định.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'should learn', 'could practise', 'would change' express suggestion, possibility, and a hypothetical result.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Answering an objection
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Some people will say… I disagree…' là khung mạnh mẽ để xử lý ý kiến trái chiều trong một bài luận quan điểm.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Some people will say… I disagree…' is a strong frame for handling a counter-opinion in a short opinion essay.

sampleTranslationVi:
Tôi nghĩ rằng mỗi học sinh nên học cách lắng nghe tử tế trước khi rời trường. Chúng ta được dạy viết luận, thuyết trình, bảo vệ ý kiến, nhưng không ai thực sự dạy ta cách ngồi trong một căn phòng và lắng nghe một người mà ta không đồng ý. Kết quả là, nhiều người lớn thì ồn ào nhưng cô đơn. Họ không thể có một cuộc trò chuyện thực sự.

Một tiết học về lắng nghe sẽ không khó. Học sinh có thể luyện tập nhắc lại điều bạn cùng lớp vừa nói trước khi trả lời. Họ có thể học cách hỏi thêm một câu thay vì chen ngang với ý kiến của mình. Theo thời gian, điều này sẽ thay đổi cách họ nói chuyện với gia đình, đồng nghiệp, và cả những người lạ trên xe buýt.

Một số người sẽ nói rằng lắng nghe là thói quen cá nhân, không phải một môn học. Tôi không đồng ý. Chúng ta học đọc dù dùng mắt mỗi ngày. Chúng ta cũng có thể học lắng nghe, dù vẫn luôn có đôi tai. Bắt đầu càng sớm, thế giới càng trở nên tử tế hơn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states an opinion most clearly and politely at B1 level?
options: Every student must learn listening, end of story.
Maybe listening is something or maybe not.
I think every student should learn how to listen well before they leave school.
Listening to be learned by every student is.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I think' + 'should' gives a clear opinion with a polite modal; the others are either rude, vague, or ungrammatical.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a clear opinion phrase combined with a modal verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence using a modal verb for a hypothetical result.
options: Over time, this would change the way they talk to others.
Over time, this will been changed how they talk to others.
Over time, this changed the way they talk to others.
Over time, this is changing they talk to others.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would + base verb' describes a hypothetical future result that follows from a proposal.
mcQuestions.hint: Which modal verb describes a likely result of a proposal?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly answers an objection?
options: Some people listening is personal habit but no.
Some people will say that listening is a personal habit, not a school subject. I disagree.
I disagree because some people, listening is personal.
Some people will say listening, but I am not agreed.
mcQuestions.explanation: State the objection in one full sentence, then respond with 'I disagree' in a new sentence.
mcQuestions.hint: An objection should be stated cleanly before you push back.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural for connecting cause and effect?
options: Result as the, many adults are loud but lonely.
As a result, many adults are loud but lonely.
As result of, many adults loud lonely.
Because as a result, many adults are loud.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'As a result' is a fixed linking phrase, used at the start of a sentence with a comma.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the option that uses 'as a result' as a standard connector at the start of a sentence.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If you could replace one subject on the school timetable with a new one, what would you remove, and what would you add? Why?
